title: Mellow Bitter Portuguese Cheese Lasagna
description: A cozy Portuguese dish featuring ground ginger and fry grind rice khao khu, with a bitter cheese pull, this lasagna is a unique and flavorful experience. It is also ayurvedic rajasic friendly.

ingredients.name: ground beef
ingredients.notes: can be substituted with ground turkey

instructions: Cook the lasagna noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the ground beef, cooking until browned.
Add the ground ginger, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the fry grind rice khao khu to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lasagna noodles, marinara sauce, and shredded cheese.
In a 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of the marinara sauce.
Add a layer of the cooked lasagna noodles on top of the sauce.
Add a layer of the ground beef and fry grind rice khao khu mixture on top of the noodles.
Repeat the layers two more times, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the lasagna in the preheated oven for 30-40 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
